AVAV.headline: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges AeroVironment, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
AVAV.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against AeroVironment, Inc. alleging that the company made materially false and misleading statements regarding its business prospects. Specifically, the complaint claims AeroVironment understated competition risks related to the SCAR program and U.S. Space Force modernization efforts, thereby overstating its financial prospects. Investors who purchased AeroVironment securities between June 25, 2025 and March 10, 2026 are eligible to join the case, with a le

AVAV.headline: Is AeroVironment Inc a Buy After Its Latest Earnings Report?
AVAV.description: AeroVironment crushed analyst estimates in fiscal 2026 Q4 with quarterly revenue doubling to $641.6M and net income surging to $63M. The company's backlog ballooned to $1.2B, and management guided for fiscal 2027 revenue between $2.13B-$2.23B. Growth was driven by both organic expansion and recent acquisitions including BlueHalo and Empirical Systems Aerospace.

AVAV.headline: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Reminds Aerovironment, Inc. Investors They Have Until July 27th to Contact the Firm Seeking Lead Plaintiff Role
AVAV.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against AeroVironment, Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding competition for its Satellite Communication Augmentation Resource (SCAR) program work. The company faced a U.S. government stop work order in January 2026, causing a 15% stock decline, followed by a contract termination announcement in March 2026 that triggered a 6.24% drop and a $151.3 million goodwill impairment.

AVAV.headline: Lead Plaintiff Deadlines in Shareholder Class Action Lawsuits Against Calix, Inc. (CALX), AeroVironment, Inc. (AVAV), and Lucid Group, Inc. (LCID) Announced by Holzer & Holzer, LLC
AVAV.description: Holzer & Holzer, LLC announced lead plaintiff deadlines for multiple shareholder class action lawsuits against Calix, Inc., AeroVironment, Inc., and Lucid Group, Inc., alleging materially false statements and non-disclosure of material facts. Investors who purchased shares during specified periods and suffered losses are encouraged to seek legal representation.

VSAT.headline: Why Viasat Stock Went to the Moon Today
VSAT.description: Rocket Lab announced an $8 billion acquisition of Iridium Communications, which sparked investor enthusiasm across the space sector. Viasat stock surged 19.74% despite not being directly involved in the deal, as investors speculate it could become a future merger target due to its valuable wireless spectrum holdings. The acquisition signals industry consolidation as smaller space companies seek to compete with SpaceX.

ALAB.headline: Even CEOs Need Cash: Insider Selling Is Not the Only Signal in AI Stocks
ALAB.description: The article argues that insider selling in AI stocks should not be viewed as a bearish signal, as executives often sell through pre-arranged trading plans for tax and diversification purposes. Instead, institutional buying and analyst sentiment are more reliable indicators. While NVIDIA, Snowflake, Astera Labs, Datadog, and CoreWeave show strong institutional support and bullish analyst trends, some stocks like Datadog and Astera Labs may be vulnerable to pullbacks due to recent sharp rallies. T

RKLB.headline: Rocket Lab Just Made a Move That Could Change Everything
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab announced a major acquisition of satellite telecom giant Iridium for $8 billion, a strategic move that could significantly reshape the company's growth trajectory. The acquisition drew comparisons to SpaceX's strategic playbook and was well-received by the market, with RKLB stock rising 2.92% on the news.

RKLB.headline: Why Viasat Stock Went to the Moon Today
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab announced an $8 billion acquisition of Iridium Communications, which sparked investor enthusiasm across the space sector. Viasat stock surged 19.74% despite not being directly involved in the deal, as investors speculate it could become a future merger target due to its valuable wireless spectrum holdings. The acquisition signals industry consolidation as smaller space companies seek to compete with SpaceX.

RKLB.headline: Why Rocket Lab Stock Went to the Moon Today
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab announced an $8 billion acquisition of Iridium Communications, paying $54 per share ($27 cash, rest in stock). The deal combines Rocket Lab's launch capabilities with Iridium's satellite constellation for IoT, aviation, maritime, and positioning services, positioning the company to compete more directly with SpaceX's Starlink business.

ASTS.headline: SpaceX Has Real Value—But These 3 Stocks Have Better Odds Right Now
ASTS.description: While SpaceX's $1.3-1.5 trillion valuation is justified by its three business segments (launch, Starlink, and xAI compute), analysts suggest better investment opportunities exist. ASML, Northrop Grumman, and GE Vernova offer more attractive risk-reward profiles in AI infrastructure, space economy, and power generation respectively. Tesla and AST SpaceMobile are viewed as overvalued relative to their growth prospects.

ASTS.headline: Buy AST SpaceMobile Before Aug. 1 Due to This Opportunity
ASTS.description: AST SpaceMobile is preparing to launch three BlueBird satellites in August 2026 with larger 2,400-square-foot antennas expected to nearly double current download speeds to ~200 Mbps. The company targets 45 satellites in orbit by end of 2026 and projects revenue growth from $14.7M in Q1 to $150-200M for full year 2026, with potential $1B revenue in 2027. With $3.5B in cash and diversified launch partnerships, the upcoming launches represent a key test of the company's ability to scale its satelli

CBRS.headline: Is Cerebras Stock a Buy on the Dip as Revenue Surges?
CBRS.description: Cerebras shares declined after reporting strong Q1 revenue growth of 92% year-over-year to $193.4 million and securing major partnerships with OpenAI ($20 billion deal) and AWS. However, investors were disappointed by gross margin guidance forecasting a decline to 38-41% for the year. Despite impressive growth projections and strategic partnerships, the stock remains highly speculative given its $40 billion market cap relative to projected sub-$900 million annual revenue.

CBRS.headline: SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Hub Group, Inc. Sued for Securities Law Violations; Investors Should Contact Block & Leviton To Learn How They Might Recover Their Losses
CBRS.description: Hub Group, Inc. faces a securities fraud lawsuit after restating financial statements due to approximately $77 million in understatements of purchased transportation costs and accounts payable. The company's stock fell 18% in February 2026 and an additional 13% in May 2026 following disclosure of material misstatements in its 2023, 2024, and 2025 financial reports. Investors who purchased shares between April 28, 2023, and May 11, 2026, may be eligible to recover losses.

CBRS.headline: Why Cerebras Stock Is Skyrocketing Today
CBRS.description: Cerebras stock surged over 20% this week following a 29% plunge the previous week, driven by Ark Invest's purchase of shares across two of its ETFs. The semiconductor company, which designs AI chips, received no company-specific news but benefited from Cathie Wood's investment endorsement. However, the article cautions investors against blindly following other investors' activity given the stock's significant volatility.

CBRS.headline: Where Will Cerebras Stock be in 3 Years?
CBRS.description: Cerebras Systems, an AI chip company, went public in May 2026 and has experienced significant volatility, losing 41% since its IPO pop. Despite disappointing Q1 2026 earnings guidance and margin concerns, analysts remain bullish with a 12-month price target of $300 (65% upside). The company's wafer-sized chips and major partnerships with OpenAI ($20B+) and Amazon position it for potential 175% upside over three years if it achieves $7.4B revenue by 2028, though its current 65x price-to-sales val

CBRS.headline: OpenAI Reportedly Considers Delaying Its IPO. Should You Worry About AI Stocks?
CBRS.description: OpenAI is reportedly considering delaying its IPO until next year to potentially achieve a $1 trillion valuation, according to The New York Times. While AI stocks have faced some pressure due to valuation concerns and high spending, the article argues investors shouldn't worry if they hold quality AI companies with strong competitive positions, as the AI growth story is likely still in early stages.

BB.headline: Stock Market Outlook: Tesla Buy Signal, BlackBerry Surges
BB.description: As markets approach the end of Q2 and H1, trading is expected to be quieter due to holiday-shortened weeks in Canada and the US. The NASDAQ is positioned as the primary market leader. Technical analysis highlights Tesla's new buy signal and BlackBerry's continued strength, while several telecom and mining stocks show weakness. Bitcoin conditions are improving.

Viasat stock surged 19.74% despite not being directly involved in the deal, as investors speculate it could become a future merger target due to its valuable wireless spectrum holdings. The acquisition signals industry consolidation as smaller space companies seek to compete with SpaceX.

GLW.headline: The AI Boom Has a Second Act—And It’s Playing Out in Optics
GLW.description: While AI infrastructure investment focuses on chips, the real opportunity lies in optical networking components. Companies manufacturing fiber, connectors, and routing systems are landing multi-billion-dollar contracts with hyperscalers and facing supply bottlenecks. Three key players—Amphenol, Corning, and Ciena—are positioned to benefit from the $1 trillion AI infrastructure buildout, with analyst estimates expected to be revised higher as demand accelerates through 2030.
